mechanism_narrative: Sustained low and stable inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, indicates a healthy economic environment without significant monetary tightening pressure, leading to stable corporate earnings and positive equity market performance over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains low and stable, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic contractions, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly positive in more than 60% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) show strong growth while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, which is highly favorable for corporate revenues and thus S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is strong and FEDFUNDS is low, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 6-month period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, indicated by a significant rise in FEDFUNDS, higher borrowing costs for corporations and consumers reduce economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises significantly over 6 months during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly positive (positive returns in > 65% of triggered instances).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong underlying demand and confidence in the economy, which typically translates into broader economic growth and positive sentiment for equity markets, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST consistently exceeds 1500k units for 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative more than 55% of the time in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and mining output, reflecting weakening demand or supply chain disruptions, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows a sustained decline over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are predominantly positive (average return > 0.2%) in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strong and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently grows by more than 0.1% on a 3-month average, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.05%) over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) suggests an overheating economy, which often prompts aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, ultimately harming equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth > 4% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 1.5%
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and RSAFS show strong growth as specified, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently positive (positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances).
